news.headline: AFL self-certifies compliance with BABA Act, key supplier for BEAD Program broadband projects.
news.summary: AFL, a prominent fiber optic cables, connectivity, and equipment manufacturer, based in Spartanburg, South Carolina, has officially self-certified its products to comply with the Build America, Buy America (BABA) Act regulations. This important certification now establishes AFL as a vital supplier for broadband infrastructure initiatives supported by the Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment (BEAD) Program. The BEAD Program falls under the administration of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration, emphasizing the significance of AFL's compliance for eligibility in contributing to projects under BEAD.

news.summary: In Philadelphia on October 18, 2024, researchers at the Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS) conducted a study revealing that patients suffering from obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) who utilized continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) therapy prior to undergoing total hip and knee replacement surgery...

news.headline: Elevance Health Inc. reported Q3 earnings, with $44.7 billion revenues and $1.02 billion profit, missing expectations.
news.summary: Elevance Health Inc. (NYSE:ELV) recently reported its third-quarter earnings, falling short of expectations. The company recorded revenues of $44.7 billion, marking a 5.3% increase from the previous year and surpassing the anticipated $43.33 billion. Despite this revenue growth, profits decreased by 21% to $1.02 billion, translating to $4.36 per share. Adjusted earnings per share also took a hit, decreasing to $8.37 from $8.99 year-over-year, missing the expected $9.66. The company expressed confidence in its various business segments' long-term earning potential amid a challenging operating environment and unique obstacles in the Medicaid sector. Stay informed with more details on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Merck's virus treatment clesrovimab is 60.4% effective in reducing RSV infections in infants as per Phase 2b/3 trial.
news.summary: Merck & Co Inc (NYSE:MRK) has shared findings from a Phase 2b/3 trial (MK-1654-004) of clesrovimab, a prophylactic monoclonal antibody aimed at safeguarding infants from respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) during their inaugural RSV season. Results and interim data from the ongoing Phase 3 trial (MK-1654-007) were disclosed at IDWeek 2024. In the Phase 2b/3 pivotal trial assessing a single clesrovimab dose given to healthy preterm and full-term infants (birth to 1 year old), all prespecified goals were achieved, showing consistent outcomes up to the 6-month mark post-administration. The assessment reported the incidence of adverse events (AEs) and demonstrated successful outcomes, contrasting GSK and Pfizer's decreasing sales of respiratory syncytial virus vaccines due to specific age limitations. For further details, please visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: US hospitals face critical IV fluid shortage after Baxter plant shutdown due to Hurricane Helene, impacting medical treatments. Baxter plant produces 60% of US IV fluids, exacerbating scarcity. Shortages include saline, sterile water, dextrose, lactated Ringer's, and peritoneal dialysis fluids. Difficulties were already present before the hurricane.
news.summary: The United States is currently grappling with a severe deficit of intravenous (IV) fluids like saline and dextrose solutions, primarily due to the impact of Hurricane Helene on Baxter International (NYSE:BAX) facilities in North Carolina. These vital fluids, already scarce prior to the hurricane, play a crucial role in various medical procedures such as hydration, wound care, and dialysis. With Baxter's plant, responsible for 60% of the nation's IV fluid production, offline, hospitals are finding it challenging to maintain ample stocks. Even before the hurricane, the U.S. was experiencing shortages of these fluids, as reported by the FDA. The deficiency of saline solution began in 2018, sterile water in 2021, and dextrose solution in early 2022. Hurricane Helene exacerbated the issue, causing shortages of lactated Ringer's solution and peritoneal dialysis fluids. This scarcity has raised concerns among healthcare providers about the availability of critical medical supplies.

news.headline: Wall Street analysts suggest buying Workday with an average recommendation of 1.65 out of 5, based on 34 firms. 67.7% recommend Strong Buy.
news.summary: The article discusses the impact of Wall Street analyst recommendations on stock prices, using Workday (NASDAQ: WDAY) as an example. Currently, Workday has an average brokerage recommendation (ABR) of 1.65, which falls between Strong Buy and Buy on a scale of 1 to 5. Out of 34 brokerage firms, 23 recommend Strong Buy and 2 recommend Buy, totaling 67.7% and 5.9% of all recommendations, respectively. However, studies show that relying solely on brokerage recommendations may not lead to successful investment decisions. The article delves into the unreliability of such recommendations in predicting stock performance.

news.headline: Blackstone Inc surpasses earnings expectations, with Q3 revenues of $2.434B, DE rising 6% to $1.279B, and $54B invested/committed.
news.summary: Total revenues for Blackstone Inc (NYSE:BX) in the third quarter of fiscal year reached $2.434 billion, surpassing the estimated $2.408 billion. Distributable earnings saw a 6% increase from the previous year, totaling $1.279 billion, while fee-related earnings rose by 5% to $1.18 billion. During this period, the company invested or committed a remarkable $54 billion, marking the highest figure in over two years. Over the last twelve months, $123 billion has been deployed since the peak of the cost of capital. CEO and Chairman Stephen A. Schwarzman highlighted that this quarter also saw the highest level of fund appreciation in three years. Blackstone's continued success has garnered trust from limited partners, maintaining its strong performance in the market.

news.headline: Global solid biomass feedstock market valued at $28.46 billion in 2024, expected to grow at a CAGR of 6.08%. Key players: Enviva, Drax, Ecostrat leading innovation for energy production & environmental conservation.
news.summary: The market for solid biomass feedstock is on the rise globally, with an optimistic projection showing a value of $28.46 billion in 2024. This growth is fueled by key factors and market drivers, leading to an anticipated compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6.08% from 2024 to 2034. The analysis covers various aspects such as applications, end-users, sources, types, and regions. news.headline: Chinese tech stocks like Alibaba (BABA) and JD.com (JD) surged after China's GDP grew 4.6% in Q3, beating estimates. Central bank support also boosted market sentiment.
news.summary: Chinese tech giants Alibaba Group Holding Ltd – ADR (NYSE:BABA), JD.Com Inc (NASDAQ:JD) and Baidu Inc (NASDAQ:BIDU) saw gains driven by stronger-than-expected GDP figures from China, with the country's economy expanding by 4.6% year-over-year in the third quarter, beating a Reuters poll forecast of 4.5%. The upbeat economic data lifted market sentiment and contributed to a rally in China-based exchange-traded funds. The People's Bank of China (PBOC) also contributed to the optimism with a support program for share buybacks, leading to further market confidence. The National Bureau of Statistics expressed confidence in potential policy actions and noted the complex external environment. Industrial output rose by 5.4%, showing resilience in the second-largest economy.
